# fix(ml): extend M2 to all 7 non-DEBUG sites + SHA-256 8-hex fingerprint

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `3267591eeec0` — fix(ml): extend M2 to all 7 non-DEBUG sites + SHA-256 8-hex fingerprint

**Body**:

Post-PR reviewer + security-reviewer flagged two issues:

1. observability.md §8 applies to INFO/WARN/ERROR, not only WARN.
   5 additional engine.py sites still leaked raw model_name at
   INFO/ERROR: evaluate.ok, evaluate.drift.no_monitor_configured,
   evaluate.drift.no_reference, engine.register.error,
   engine.register.ok. All now emit model_name_fingerprint at their
   original log level + partition sibling logger.debug <event>.detail
   carrying the raw model_name.

2. Python's hash() & 0xFFFF:04x is PYTHONHASHSEED-randomized
   (different per process — defeats cross-process log-aggregator
   correlation) AND 4-hex is inconsistent with the cross-SDK
   contract in event-payload-classification.md §2 (8-hex SHA-256).

Fix: _hash_model_name() helper at engine.py top emits the canonical
hashlib.sha256(name.encode()).hexdigest()[:8] form. Both WARN sites
(audit_write_failed, onnx_partial_failure) and the 5 new INFO/ERROR
sites route through the helper.

M2 regression test rewrite: AST invariant walks ALL non-DEBUG levels;
DEBUG-sibling guard covers all 7 sites; fingerprint-format test
asserts 8-hex SHA-256 + deterministic across calls + matches the
helper. NEW test locks the 7-site scope so a future refactor
removing a fingerprint-emitting site fails loudly.

M1 test: 4-hex → 8-hex + hex-charset validation.
L2 test: path resolution now cwd-independent (Path(__file__) relative).

15/15 regression tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
